title: diphyllocalyx cayensis subsp. cayensis
intro: Diphyllocalyx cayensis subsp. cayensis, also known as Cayenne pepper tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain due to its high concentration of capsaicin. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and even alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Diphyllocalyx cayensis subsp. cayensis are made by drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or by mixing the dried plant material with other herbs to create a topical ointment or salve.
